title: Nanobind Extraction Protocol for Easily Fragmenting DNA
author: Giada Ferrari

steps.title: Cut a suitable piece of frozen...
steps.instruction: Cut a suitable piece of frozen tissue with a scalpel blade in a pre-cooled petri dish or weighing boat. Work on ice or dry ice.

steps.title: Remove the sample from the ice...
steps.instruction: Remove the sample from the ice and immediately cover with 1-5 mL of L buffer (from Sambrook and Russel, Molecular Cloning: A laboratory Manual, 2001). Buffer L 1 10 mM Tris pH 7.6 2 100 mM EDTA 3 20 mM NaCl Recipe for Buffer L from Sambrook and Russel, Molecular Cloning: A laboratory Manual, 2001

steps.title: Allow the sample to thaw in the L buffer
steps.instruction: Allow the sample to thaw in the L buffer.

steps.title: Mince the tissue into small pieces with a scalpel blade
steps.instruction: Mince the tissue into small pieces with a scalpel blade.

steps.title: Collect the buffer and tissue pieces into microcentrifuge tubes
steps.instruction: Collect the buffer and tissue pieces into microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: If necessary, rinse the petri dish...
steps.instruction: If necessary, rinse the petri dish with L buffer and collect into the same tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 3,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 3,000 x g for 00:05:00 and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Use a total of 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Use a total of 1 mL of cold Buffer CT to resuspend the pellets by pipette mixing 10X with a wide bore P200 pipette.

steps.title: Transfer all the resuspended pellets into...
steps.instruction: Transfer all the resuspended pellets into one 2 mL Protein LoBind microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Pellet homogenate by centrifuging at 3,000...
steps.instruction: Pellet homogenate by centrifuging at 3,000 x g and 4°C for 00:05:00 . Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Pulse vortex for 1s x 2...
steps.instruction: Pulse vortex for 1s x 2 times (max setting) to dislodge pellet.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K to the previous pellet
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K to the previous pellet.

steps.title: Add 150 µL of Buffer CLE3...
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL of Buffer CLE3 and pipette mix 10X with a wide bore P200 pipette.

steps.title: Incubate on a ThermoMixer at 55°C...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a ThermoMixer at 55°C and 600 rpm for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge for 2 s to remove liquid from the cap.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of RNase A
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of RNase A.

steps.title: Incubate on a ThermoMixer at 55°C...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a ThermoMixer at 55°C and 600 rpm for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge for 2 s to remove liquid from the cap.

steps.title: Add 60 µL of Buffer SB...
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL of Buffer SB and pulse vortex for 1s x 5 times (max setting) to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g and RT (15–30°C) for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g and RT (15–30°C) for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Transfer up to 300 µL of...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 300 µL of supernatant to a new 1.5 mL Protein LoBind microcentrifuge tube using a wide bore P200 pipette. (Discard the 2 mL Protein LoBind microcentrifuge tube containing the precipitated pellet.)

steps.title: Add 50 µL of Buffer BL3...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of Buffer BL3 to the previous supernatant and inversion mix 10X.

steps.title: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge for 2 s to remove liquid from the cap.

steps.title: Add Nanobind disk to lysate and...
steps.instruction: Add Nanobind disk to lysate and add 350 µL of isopropanol. Inversion mix 10X.

steps.title: Mix on a platform rocker at...
steps.instruction: Mix on a platform rocker at 20 rpm for 00:15:00 at RT.

steps.title: Place tube rack on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place tube rack on the magnetic base using the method described in the Nanobind tissue kit Guide & overview “Magnetic rack handling procedure” section (PacBio).

steps.title: Discard supernatant with a pipette, taking...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant with a pipette, taking care to avoid pipetting the DNA or contacting the Nanobind disk.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer CW1,...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer CW1, remove tube rack from magnetic base, inversion mix 4X, replace the tube rack on the magnetic base, and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat CW1 wash
steps.instruction: Repeat CW1 wash.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Buffer CW2,...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Buffer CW2, remove tube rack from magnetic base, inversion mix 4X, replace the tube rack on the magnetic base and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat CW2 wash
steps.instruction: Repeat CW2 wash.

steps.title: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge for 2 s
steps.instruction: Spin the tube on a mini-centrifuge for 2 s. With the tube rack already on the magnetic base and right-side-up, place tube on tube rack and remove residual liquid.

steps.title: Repeat previous step
steps.instruction: Repeat previous step.

steps.title: Remove tube from tube rack
steps.instruction: Remove tube from tube rack.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Buffer LTE...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Buffer LTE directly onto the Nanobind disk and incubate at RT for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Collect DNA by transferring eluate to a new 1
steps.instruction: Collect DNA by transferring eluate to a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Spin the tube containing the Nanobind...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube containing the Nanobind disk on a mini-centrifuge for 5 s. Use a standard P200 pipette to combine any additional liquid that comes off the disk with the previous eluate. Repeat if necessary.

steps.title: Pipette mix the sample 5X with...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix the sample 5X with a wide-bore 200 µL pipette tip to homogenize and disrupt any unsolubilized “jellies” that may be present.

steps.title: Let sample rest at RT overnight...
steps.instruction: Let sample rest at RT overnight to allow DNA to solubilize.
